name: Adelaide Hall
abstract: Adelaide Louise Hall (20 October 1901 – 7 November 1993) was an American-born UK-based jazz singer and entertainer. Her career spanned more than 70 years from 1921 until her death.

concerts.label: Adelaide Hall: A Potpourri of Cabaret plus A Tribute to Duke Ellington

works.t: When a Woman Loves a Man
When a Woman Loves a Man

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Art often told me the story of he was appearing with Adelaide Hall as her accompanist

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded with Willie Lewis and George Johnson was also engaged for this date which augmented Willie's orchestra to 11 pieces, band plus two singers, one of which was Adelaide Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I became quite friendly with Adelaide Hall... I became very friendly with her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Eldridge
about_quotes.evidence: I don't think. But the way the story goes is, he came...I think Joe Turner, the piano player who was with Adelaide Hall, and they got Art on second piano, so she had two pianists accompanying her.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: we found some people like Adelaide Hall, Margaret Simms... Adelaide Hall '41 set the stage up for the band... She came on with her old uku-lele and held the curtain.

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: He was with playing with this Adelaide something... Adelaide Hall I think I believe. Whoever it was, that introduced him into New York.
